title: Novatrooper
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Organization

'''Novatroopers''', also known as '''Nova-class Stormtroopers''', were a specialized variant of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] who were assigned to significant locations such as [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Homeworld/Legends|birthworld]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. Novatroopers were somewhat better trained than the average stormtrooper, but they were similarly equipped. Novatroopers were distinguished by their black and [[Gold/Legends|gold]] [[Color/Legends|colored]] reinforced [[Stormtrooper armor/Legends|stormtrooper armor]], but different Novatrooper variants existed. Novatrooper [[Medic/Legends|medics]] wore black and red [[Armor/Legends|armor]], and other Novatroopers wore all black armor, doubling in their duties as [[Shadow stormtrooper/Legends|shadow stormtroopers]]. Although Novatroopers at [[Time/Legends|times]] participated in patrols to crackdown on [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] activity, and were involved in a number of invasions, they were rarely encountered outside of official Imperial ceremonies.

==History==
{{Quote|Imperial forces are known to have captured many Alliance corvettes. Highly trained Novatroopers are the primary Imperial troops used to board and take control of Rebel starships. Infiltrate an Imperial controlled Corellian corvette and defeat four Elite Novatrooper Commanders.|An unidentified voice, instructing a Force-sensitive individual|<ref name="AED" />}}
By [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="IH">''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]''</ref> Novatroopers,<ref name="AED" /> also known as Nova-class [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]],<ref name="Publish7">{{SonyCite|url=recent_updates.jsp?o=61441|text=Publish 7: Droid Invasion}}</ref> served as stormtrooper honor guards in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]],<ref name="Rebellion" /> and were listed in the ''[[Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]'', an official [[Book/Legends|guidebook]] for strategies, branches, units, [[Philosopher/Legends|philosophy]], and [[Historian/Legends|history]] for the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]].<ref name="IH" /> Novatroopers were typically assigned to historically significant locations such as [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Homeworld/Legends|birthworld]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]].<ref name="Rebellion" /> For the most part, Novatroopers were rarely encountered outside of official Imperial ceremonies, such as the [[Standard year/Legends|semi-annual]] awarding of the [[Medal of Valor/Legends|Medal of Valor]] and the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor's]] public address to the citizens of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] at the start of [[New Year Fete Week/Legends|New Year's]] [[Fete Week]].<ref name="IH" />

However, due to a surge in [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] activity after their victory at the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]],<ref name="GCWForum">{{SonyCite|url=content.jsp?page=Friday%20Feature%20GCW%20Forum|text=The Galactic Civil War Forum}}</ref> Elite Novatroopers were deployed to support crackdowns on illegal activity. After successful operations, the Imperial Novatroopers were subsequently relieved of their duties.<ref name="Publish 8">{{SonyCite|url=recent_updates.jsp?o=61943|text=Publish 8: Droids Rebuilt}}</ref> Regardless, Novatroopers continued to ocasionally patrol other areas across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] during the course of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="AED" /> Novatroopers also guarded captured enemy [[Starship/Legends|starships]],<ref name="Rebellion" /> including a number of [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvettes]].<ref name="AED" />

In one diabolically crafted [[Politics/Legends|political]] maneuver, the Empire stole a Rebel [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] and filled it with explosives. Their intention was to send the craft [[Ramming/Legends|hurtling]] into [[Centerpoint Station]] near [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]. This false flag attack was intended to make the Rebels look both foolish and dangerous. Novatroopers were responsible for securing the inner corridors of the corvette, while the Empire spent [[Time/Legends|time]] rigging the ship with explosives.<ref name="AED" />

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars Galaxies}}
They were thwarted, however, by the efforts of [[Master Sergeant/Legends|Master Sergeant]] [[Crowley (Twi'lek)|Crowley]], who dispatched a team of Rebels to board the craft and [[Mission to destroy a captured Rebel CR90 corvette|destroy]] it before it could carry out its mission. In another dastardly plot, Novatroopers led by a ruthless Imperial [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]]<ref name="AED" /> overtook a Rebel [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] runner, eliminated the entire crew, and planned to crash the ship into a suspected Rebel [[City/Legends|city]] on the surface of [[Tralus/Legends|Tralus]].<ref name="Corvette">{{SonyCite|url=content.jsp?page=The%20Corellian%20Corvette|text=The Corellian Corvette}}</ref> They were again stopped, this time by the [[Mission to assassinate an Imperial Inquisitor|efforts]] of a team of Rebels sent by [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Pashna Starkiller (Mon Calamari)|Pashna Starkiller]]. The Empire also used Novatroopers<ref name="AED" /> to seize control of a corvette belonging to the [[Corellian Security Force]]. Using the craft as a [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]] [[Transport/Legends|transport]],<ref name="Corvette" /> Novatroopers remained onboard to guard [[Gabriel Ian]], a highly-decorated [[Rebel pilot/Legends|Alliance pilot]]<ref name="AED" /> that CorSec [[Officer/Legends|officers]] arrested under suspicion of [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorist]] activities.<ref name="Corvette" /> However, the Novatroopers failed to adequately guard their prisoner. [[Adar Tallon/Legends|Adar Tallon]] of the Rebel Alliance successfully oversaw a [[Mission to rescue Gabriel Ian|mission]] to board the vessel and rescue Ian before he could be transported to the [[Dathomir Imperial Prison|Imperial Prison]] on [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]]. During the time of the Galactic Civil War, a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] individual [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] in the ways of the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side of the Force]] [[Meditation/Legends|meditated]] at a [[The Force/Legends|Force]] shrine. In the course of doing so, the Force wielder heard an unidentified voice instructing the individual to infiltrate an Imperial controlled Corellian corvette and [[Death/Legends|kill]] four Elite Novatrooper [[Commander/Legends|Commanders]].<ref name="AED" /> When Rebel forces besieged the cities of [[Bestine (city)/Legends|Bestine]], [[Dearic/Legends|Dearic]], and [[Keren/Legends|Keren]]<ref name="Update 15">{{SonyCite|url=players/news_archive.vm?id=68602&month=012010|text=''Star Wars'' Galaxies™ Galactic Civil War: Forces Under Siege (Game Update 15)}}</ref> in the years leading up to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="SWGDate">According to its [[opening crawl]], ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'' was set sometime after [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. One character in the [[Star Wars video games|game]], [[Ruwan Tokai]], referenced the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|destruction]] of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] as occurring one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] earlier, placing the timeline somewhere around [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]]. However, the existence of other characters and quests in the game indicated a timeline of anywhere from [[0 ABY/Legends|0]] to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].</ref> Elite Novatrooper Commanders were involved in both defending the cities from Rebel invasions, and driving out enemy occupiers.<ref name="AED" />
{{Endgame}}

==Organization==
[[File:NovatrooperCaptain.jpg|220px|thumb|A Novatrooper Captain onboard a captured Corellian corvette]]
Specially trained and equipped for difficult assaults, Novatroopers were among the best of the Empire's ground [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]]. The most skilled within their [[Military rank/Legends|ranks]] were regarded as truly terrifying in action.<ref name="Card">{{SWGTCG|set=Champions of the Force (SWGTCG)|cardname=Elite Novatrooper}}</ref> Predominantly comprised of [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|males]],<ref name="AED" /> Novatroopers belonged to the [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]], a military branch which supported both the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] and [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Navy]].<ref name="Essential" /> However, a number of Novatroopers also doubled as [[Shadow stormtrooper/Legends|shadow stormtroopers]],<ref name="AED" /> [[Special forces/Legends|specialized]] stormtroopers which served in [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]]<ref name="Rebellion" /> under [[Director of Imperial Intelligence|Director]] [[Cronal]].<ref name="GW" /> Novatrooper [[Operating number/Legends|operating numbers]] were preceded by either TK, GK, RK, LK, or VK. They were also segmented into varying ranks and positions. Standard Novatroopers, Novatrooper [[Cadet/Legends|Cadets]], Novatrooper [[Ensign/Legends|Ensigns]], and Novatrooper [[Squadron Leader/Legends|Squad Leaders]] were among those who doubled as shadow stormtroopers, as indicated by their entirely [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Armor/Legends|armor]]. However, Novatrooper [[Captain/Legends|Captains]] and Commanders also wore all black armor, but they did not double as such. Likewise, Elite Novatroopers and Elite Novatrooper Commanders, who were distinguished by their [[Gold/Legends|gold]] markings, were not designated as shadow troopers. Novatrooper [[Medic/Legends|Medics]] were unique, in that they wore black armor with red markings. They were also assigned to captured CR90 corvettes and wilderness patrol areas in limited numbers. [[Dark Novatrooper]]s were a type of [[Dark Trooper/Legends|dark trooper]] which bore the Novatrooper name,<ref name="AED" /> deadly next-generation [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] used by the Galactic Empire.<ref name="DarkForces">''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]''</ref> These Dark Novatrooper [[Droid/Legends|droids]] accompanied their Human counterparts aboard captured vessels, as well as during the sieges of Bestine, Dearic, and Keren. [[Nova Trooper Commando]]s were another variant, however, they donned entirely different equipment.<ref name="AED" />

==Equipment==
[[File:NovatrooperMedic.png|140px|thumb|A Novatrooper Medic in black and red armor]]
{{Quote|You will recognize an Elite Novatrooper Commander by their black armor with a golden pauldron.|An unidentified voice, to a Force-sensitive individual|<ref name="AED" />}}
[[E-11 carbine]]s were the primary weapon of choice used by Novatroopers, however they were also armed with [[DLT-20A blaster rifle/Legends|DLT-20A blaster rifles]], [[DX-2 disruptor pistol/Legends|DX-2 disruptor pistols]],<ref name="AED" /> and [[Fragmentation grenade/Legends|fragmentation grenades]]. Novatroopers wore black reinforced [[Stormtrooper armor/Legends|stormtrooper armor]].<ref name="Rebellion" /> Depending on their rank and position, the armor would be colored with certain markings or affixed with specific [[Pauldron/Legends|pauldrons]]. Standard Novatroopers, Novatrooper Cadets, Novatrooper Ensigns, and Novatrooper [[Squad/Legends|Squad]] Leaders wore entirely black armor, however the squad leaders were differentiated by a dark orange-colored pauldron on their right [[Limb/Legends|shoulder]]. These specific Novatroopers also doubled as shadow stormtroopers, otherwise known as Blackhole stormtroopers.<ref name="AED" /> Shadow stormtroopers were outfitted with [[Stygian-triprismatic polymer/Legends|stygian-triprismatic polymer]] armor.<ref name="IH" /> Novatrooper Captains and Novatrooper Commanders also wore all black armor, but they were not recognized as shadow stormtroopers. The captains were distinguished by bright orange pauldrons, while the commanders wore yellow pauldrons. Elite Novatroopers and Elite Novatrooper Commanders wore black armor, albeit with special gold markings to indicate their elite status. The Elite Novatrooper Commanders were also equipped with yellow-colored pauldrons. Like their counterparts, Novatrooper Medics wore all black armor, but with special red markings to indicate their status as medics.<ref name="AED" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[W:c:swg:Novatrooper|Novatroopers]] first appeared as non-player characters in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars Galaxies|''Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided'']], a [[Roleplaying game|massively multiplayer online role-playing game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]], prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="AED" /><ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> They made their official debut on [[April 13]], [[2004]] as part of the [[W:c:swg:Publish 7|Publish 7 Droid Invasion]] update, which introduced a new series of [[W:c:swg:Instance|instanced]] [[W:c:swg:Instanced dungeon|dungeon]] [[W:c:swg:Quest|quests]] featuring the [[W:c:swg:Guide to the Corellian Corvette|Corellian corvette]].<ref name="Publish7" /> Nine quests were added in total, including three destroy missions, three [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] missions, and three rescue missions for [[W:c:swg:Galactic Empire|Imperial]], [[W:c:swg:Rebel Alliance|Rebel]], and neutral players. Novatroopers were featured in the Rebel versions of the quests, where they could be found patrolling the corridors of the captured corvettes.<ref name="AED" /> As part of the [[W:c:swg:Publish 8 Notes for 27 April 04|Publish 8 Droids Rebuilt]] update on [[April 27]], 2004, Elite Novatrooper NPCs were deployed as part of the ongoing "Imperial Crackdown" updates, "To support the crackdown on illegal activity." However, only a day later, on [[April 28]], the Novatrooper NPCs were removed from the "Imperial Crackdown" operations during another [[W:c:swg:Update Notes: 28 April 04|update]]. The patch notes provided an [[in-universe]] explanation for their departure, by stating, "After successful operations, the Imperial Novatroopers have been relieved of their duties in the Imperial Crackdown."<ref name="Publish 8" /> Regardless, Novatrooper NPCs could still occasionally be spotted in various areas throughout the galaxy during the course of the game's lifespan.<ref name="AED" />

As part of a series of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] trials included in the game prior to the release<ref name="AED" /> of the [[W:c:swg:New Game Enhancements|New Game Enhancements]] on [[November 15]], [[2005]],<ref name="NGE">{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=64886&resource=publish|text=Publish Archive 2005}}</ref> [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] players could receive a quest from various [[W:c:swg:Force shrine|Force shrines]] to defeat four Elite Novatrooper Commanders.<ref name="AED" /> Following the release of the ''[[Star Wars Galaxies Trading Card Game]]'' [[Champions of the Force (SWGTCG)|''Champions of the Force'']] on [[August 19]], [[2008]], an [[W:c:swg:Elite Novatrooper (Card)|Elite Novatrooper]] was introduced as a game card, the artwork for which was completed by [[Monte Moore]].<ref name="Card" /><ref name="CreditsTCG">{{SonyCite|url=tradingcardgame/help/credits.vm|text=''Star Wars Galaxies'' TCG Credits}}</ref> When the developers [[W:c:swg:Galactic Civil War Update|overhauled]] the [[W:c:swg:Galactic Civil War|Galactic Civil War]] system as part of [[W:c:swg:Game Update 15|Game Update 15]] on [[January 15]], [[2010]], Novatroopers were once again featured.<ref name="SOEBtS">{{SonyCite|url=players/news_archive.vm?id=68589&month=012010|text=Friday Feature – Galactic Civil War: Game Update 15 (Behind the Scenes)}}</ref> This time, players could help deploy Novatroopers in the defense of, or attacks on three designated invasion cities, including [[W:c:swg:Bestine|Bestine]], [[W:c:swg:Dearic|Dearic]], and [[W:c:swg:Keren|Keren]]. Although players could refrain from participating in quests and objectives pertaining to the Novatroopers, this article assumes 100% game completion.<ref name="AED" />

Curiously, some Novatroopers in the game, when examined, revealed a descriptor which read, "[[W:c:swg:Black Hole Stormtrooper|black hole stormtrooper]]." Specifically, only basic Novatroopers, Novatrooper Cadets, Novatrooper Ensigns, and Novatrooper Squad Leaders had this descriptor, while the others did not. It is unclear to what extent this should be regarded as [[canon]]ical in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity, or if it should simply be considered a categorization error.<ref name="AED" /> Later sources, like the [[2009]] ''[[Rebellion Era Campaign Guide]]'',<ref name="Rebellion" /> and the [[2014]] ''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]'' seem to regard Novatroopers and shadow [[W:c:swg:Imperial Stormtrooper|stormtroopers]] as two distinct types of stormtroopers, without any explicit link between the two.<ref name="IH" /> Furthermore, shadow stormtroopers appeared elsewhere throughout [[W:c:swg:Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided|''Star Wars Galaxies'']], but none were referred to as Novatroopers.<ref name="AED" />
